chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – The supplication for the illnesses and diseases

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/433)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/433)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ عَلِّمْنِي دُعَاءً أَدْعُو بِهِ لِوَجَعٍ أَصَابَنِي قَالَ قُلْ وَ أَنْتَ سَاجِدٌ.

يَا اللَّهُ يَا رَحْمَانُ يَا رَحِيمُ يَا رَبَّ الْأَرْبَابِ وَ إِلَهَ الْآلِهَةِ وَ يَا مَلِكَ الْمُلُوكِ وَ يَا سَيِّدَ السَّادَةِ اشْفِنِي بِشِفَائِكَ مِنْ كُلِّ دَاءٍ وَ سُقْمٍ فَإِنِّي عَبْدُكَ أَتَقَلَّبُ فِي قَبْضَتِكَ

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Amro Bin usman, from Ali Bin Isa, from his uncle who said,
‘I said to himasws, ‘Teach me a supplication I can supplicate with for the pain which has hit me’. Heasws said: ‘Say while you are performing Sajdah,

‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! O Beneficent! O Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of the lords and Sustainer of the Sustainers, and O King of the kings, and O Chief of the chiefs! Heal me by Your<sup>azwj</sup> Healing, from every illness and disease, for I am Your<sup>azwj</sup> servant, turning over in Your<sup>azwj</sup> Grip’’.
en.sarwar: 11. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Amr ibn ‘Uthman from Ali ibn ‘Isa from his uncle who has said the following: “I asked him (the Imam) to teach me a supplication when I suffer from pain. He said during Sajdah (prostration) say, ‘O Allah, O the Beneficent, O the Merciful, O Lord of lords, O Lord of gods (things worshipped), O the King of kings, O Master of masters, cure me with Your treatment from all ailments and diseases; I am Your servant who moves back and forth in Your control.’”
